---
format: pdf
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
title-block-style: plain
prefer-html: true
---


# Assigned Reading & Viewing

::: {.callout-important}

Topics & readings are subject to change based on student interests and current events; changes will be announced via Canvas. *Please read or watch all assigned material before class.*

:::


## Week  1  {.unnumbered}

#### Thursday: Course Introduction  {.unnumbered}

***Reading/Viewing:*** None

## Week  2    {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Historical Narratives  {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To introduce and discuss how historical narratives by the first Europeans to visit the tropics have shaped contemporary perceptions of tropical rain forests and the colonial roots of tropical biology_  

***Reading/Viewing:*** Excerpts from Historical Narratives ([read online](https://github.com/BrunaLab/wis2323_syllabus/blob/master/readings/historical_narratives.pdf){target="_blank"}, 6 pages)

#### Thursday: Historical Narratives (cont.) {.unnumbered}

***Reading/Viewing:*** None


## Week  3   {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Rain Forest Imagery in Art & Literature {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To see how the different depictions of the tropics and tropical biodiversity from early European explorers are reflected in art and literature._

***Reading/Viewing:*** Excerpts from Literature/Poetry ([link](https://brunalab.github.io/wis2323_syllabus/readings/trf_in_lit_and_poetry.pdf){target="_blank"}, 5 pages) and images of artwork ([link](https://github.com/BrunaLab/wis2323_syllabus/blob/master/readings/tropical_paintings.pdf){target="_blank"},5 pages)


#### Thursday: The Rain Forest in Pop Culture {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To compare the use and presentation of rain forest images by the private sector and in different forms of popular culture, including the film and music industries, and to evaluate how these depictions influence perceptions of tropical countries & people._


***Reading/Viewing:*** Jolly, Priscilla. (2021). 'Godzilla vs. Kong': Monster movies evoke adventure but also 'dangers' of tropics. _The Conversation_. ([link](https://theconversation.com/godzilla-vs-kong-monster-movies-evoke-adventure-but-also-dangers-of-tropics-158105); 4 pages)


## Week  4  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: What is a Rain Forest?  {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn the different ways biologists define the "tropics" and how the structure and dynamics of tropical rain forests differ from those of forests in other parts of the world._


***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Why Does Earth Have Deserts? ([link to video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T6Us1sPXBfA); 2 min) 

1. BBC Planet Earth: Jungles. (2006). [link to video](https://video.alexanderstreet.com/watch/jungles); requires login with Gatorlink, 51 min).

<!-- Emma Napper, British Broadcasting Corporation, ZDF, T., Tencent, & France Télévisions (Producers), & Napper, E. (Director). (2016). Jungles. \[Video/DVD) BBC Worldwide. Retrieved from <https://video.alexanderstreet.com/watch/Jungles-2>. -->

<!-- We will be using the following videos for our in-class activity, you can get a head start by watching them before class. -->

<!-- [Longleaf Pine Forest] (3 minutes) -->

<!-- [360 Rain Forest] (11 minutes) -->

<!-- [Amazon video 1] (1 minutes)   -->

<!-- [Amazon video 2] (1 minutes)    -->

<!-- [Amazon video 3] (1 minutes)   -->

#### Thursday: Where is a Rain Forest? {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To understand the geological history of tropical rain forests, how climate, fire, and geological history drive the tipping point between forests and savannas, and how this biogeographic, geological, and climatic history shaped the evolution of tropical plants and animals._

***Reading/Viewing:*** None



## Week  5  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Patterns of Biodiversity {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To observe and catalog the diversity of plant and animal life forms that can be found in rain forests, to quantify the local patterns of species richness and abundance in a tropical forest, and compare these patterns with those in the temperate zone._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. CBS News. (2023). Inside the Smithsonian's tropical forest lab.  ([watch online](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-p12cyHJZf4), 5:35 min.)

1. Butler, Rhett. (2015). How many tree species are found in the world’s rainforests? _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2015/06/how-many-tree-species-are-found-in-the-worlds-rainforests/), 5 min.)

<!-- STRI “The queen of the forest” https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XI2TO7fFGi0 -->
<!-- - Ingrid Kvale, & British Broadcasting Corporation (Producers), & Kvale, I. (Director). (2021). Borneo: Sacred Forest. \[Video/DVD) BBC Worldwide. Retrieved from <https://video.alexanderstreet.com/watch/borneo-sacred-forest> -->

<!-- ForestPlotsTVTaking the pulse of Earth’s tropical forests using networks of highly distributed plots https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SFM1tVziIk0 -->


#### Thursday: Patterns of Biodiversity, continued {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To understand global patterns of species richness and how these vary from the tropics to the temperate zone._

***Reading/Viewing:*** Matthew Earl Boone and Mathieu Basille. Using iNaturalist to contribute your nature observations to science. UF EDIS Document WEC413 ([read online](https://journals.flvc.org/edis/article/view/107698/110114), 4 pages) and then familiarize yourself with `iNaturalist` at <https://www.inaturalist.org/>


## Week  6  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: The Origins of Tropical Biodiversity {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To review hypothesized mechanisms for the origins of tropical diversity and the role of interspecific interactions in the (co)evolution and diversification of tropical biodiversity_

***Reading/Viewing:*** None

#### Thursday: The maintenance of tropical biodiversity {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To review the biotic and abiotic mechanisms in tropical rain forests that permit the coexistence of so many species_

***Reading/Viewing:*** None

## Week  7  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Humans in Rain Forests {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To understand the history of human occupation of rain forests including the contemporary demographic transition from rural to urban occupation; to review the different ways in which humans have historically modified rain forests and how this has shaped current rain forest biodiversity._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Allington, Kristine, Michael Amundson, Linithd Aparicio, Caitlin Saks (Producers), and G. Townsley (Director). (2023). Ancient Builders of the Amazon. Public Broadcasting Service. ([watch online](https://video.alexanderstreet.com/watch/ancient-builders-of-the-amazon), 53 min.)

1. Hunt, Chris and Premathilake, Rathnasiri. (2018). Prehistoric people started to spread domesticated bananas across the world 6,000 years ago. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/prehistoric-people-started-to-spread-domesticated-bananas-across-the-world-6-000-years-ago-99547), 3 pages).


<!-- MAKE NARRATIVES REVISTED PART OF THE MOVIE REVIEW ASSIGNMENT? -->

#### Thursday: Humans in Rain Forests, continued {.unnumbered}

***Reading/Viewing:*** None

## Week  8  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Paradox of Luxuriance {.unnumbered}  

<!-- & (Bio)Narratives Revisited {.unnumbered}   -->

_**Goal:** To understand how such a productive biome can be built on such low-quality soils, and explore the implications of this "Paradox of Luxuriance"._

<!-- - Langewiesche, William. (2022). The War for the Rainforest. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2022/03/16/magazine/amazon-rainforest-ituna-itata.html)) -->

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Anthony Bourdain's Parts Unknown: Congo (S1E8) ([watch online](https://play.hbomax.com/video/watch/09e513a5-4bdd-4dfc-80c2-4cbe9ddb2763/d747186e-04f5-4f7a-91e2-7d3ec37e6038), 50 min).

1. Brooks, David. (2010). The Messiah Complex. _The New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2010/01/08/opinion/08brooks.html), 1 page)

<!-- https://play.hbomax.com/video/watch/09e513a5-4bdd-4dfc-80c2-4cbe9ddb2763/d747186e-04f5-4f7a-91e2-7d3ec37e6038 -->

<!-- - Bourdain's Field Notes: Congo [[read online](https://explorepartsunknown.com/congo/bourdains-field-notes-congo/), 5 min read]. -->

<!-- - Rose, Steve. 2016. "From Tarzan to Avatar: the problem with 'the white man in the jungle'". The Guardian ([link](https://www.theguardian.com/film/2016/jul/06/why-the-white-man-in-the-jungle-film-wont-die)) (newspaper story, 5-10 min. read) -->


<!-- 1. ***Watch:*** John Maggio, Hannah Olson, Ark Media, John Maggio Productions, & American Experience Films (Producers), & Maggio, J. (Director). (2018). Into the Amazon. [Video/DVD] Public Broadcasting Service. Retrieved from https://video.alexanderstreet.com/watch/into-the-amazon -->


#### Thursday: Jungle Film Festival  {.unnumbered}

***Reading/Viewing:*** None


## Week  9  {.unnumbered}


#### Tuesday: How much tropical rain forest is there? {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn how forest cover is defined and estimated and how it varies globally._

***Reading/Viewing:***  Louis Lucero. (2013). New Interactive Tool Helps Track Earth’s Forests. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2013/11/15/science/earth/new-interactive-tool-helps-track-earths-forests.html), 10 min read).

#### Thursday: How much tropical rain forest have we lost? {.unnumbered}  

_**Goal:** To use forest cover data to estimate rates of tropical forest loss over time._

***Reading/Viewing:*** Serkez, Yaryna. (2020). Every Place Under Threat. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2020/10/02/opinion/amazon-under-threat.html), 10 min read\).


<!-- - Manuela Andreoni 2023 "Despite Global Pledges, Tree Loss Is Up Sharply in Tropical Forests" ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2023/06/27/climate/trees-tropical-forests-deforestation.html), 10 min read\) -->

<!-- - Nolen, Stephanie (Reporting) with Elkaim, Aaron Vincent (Photographs). (2018). Inside the Amazon's Deforestation Crisis". The Globe and Mail. ([read online](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/news/world/amazon-rainforest-deforestation-crisis/article37722932/), 20 min read). -->


## Week  10  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Drivers & Ecological Consequences of Deforestation {.unnumbered}  

_**Goal:** Learn (a) how deforestation and other human activities alter the structure & functioning of rain forests, and (b) compare how these drivers differ between the African, American, and Asian tropics._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 


1. Devouring the Rain Forest. _Washington Post_. ([read online](https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/interactive/2022/amazon-beef-deforestation-brazil/), 20 min read).

1. Searcey, Dionne with photographs by Ashley Gilbertson. (2022). Raft by Raft, a Rainforest Loses Its Trees _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2022/06/14/climate/congo-rainforest-logging.html), 10 min read).

1. Andreoni, Manuela, Blacki Migliozzi, Pablo Robles and Denise Lu with photographs by Victor Moriyama. (2022). The Illegal Airstrips Bringing Toxic Mining to Brazil's Indigenous Land. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2022/08/02/world/americas/brazil-airstrips-illegal-mining.html), 15 min read).


#### Thursday: Drivers & Ecological Consequences of Deforestation, continued {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** Continue learning (a) how deforestation and other human activities alter the structure & functioning of rain forests, and (b) compare how these drivers differ between the African, American, and Asian tropics._


<!-- - Robles, Pablo, Anuradha Raghu, Adam Majendie and Jin Wu (2021). The World's Addiction to Palm Oil Is Only Getting Worse". Bloomberg News. ([read online](https://www.bloomberg.com/graphics/2021-palm-oil-deforestation-climate-change/), 10 min read). -->

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Mason, Margie & McDowell, Robin. (2020). Palm oil labor abuses linked to world's top brands, banks. _Associated Press_. ([read online](https://apnews.com/article/virus-outbreak-only-on-ap-indonesia-financial-markets-malaysia-7b634596270cc6aa7578a062a30423bb), 10 min read).

1. Lustgarten, Abrahm. (2018). Palm Oil Was Supposed to Help Save the Planet. Instead It Unleashed a Catastrophe. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2018/11/20/magazine/palm-oil-borneo-climate-catastrophe.html), 20 min.)


## Week  11  {.unnumbered}


#### Tuesday: Tropical Forests & Global Climate {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** Climate change and Tropical Forests To understand the relationship between tropical forests, deforestation, and the global climate cycle._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. BBC News: Amazon rainforest: 'Once it's gone, it's gone forever' - interview with Erika Berenguer. ([link to video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=TigV80hwebg), 3 min).

1. Pearce, Fred. (2018). Rivers in the Sky: How Deforestation Is Affecting Global Water Cycles. _Yale360_. ([read online](https://e360.yale.edu/features/how-deforestation-affecting-global-water-cycles-climate-change), 10 min read).


#### Thursday: Tropical Forests & Climate Change {.unnumbered}


***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Henry Knight Lozano (2023) California and Florida grew quickly on the promise of perfect climates in the 1900s – today, they lead the country in climate change risks. _The Conversation_. [Link](https://theconversation.com/california-and-florida-grew-quickly-on-the-promise-of-perfect-climates-in-the-1900s-today-they-lead-the-country-in-climate-change-risks-207470)

1. learn about the EN-ROADS simulator we will be using in class at this ([link to website](https://www.climateinteractive.org/en-roads/), 15 min read). You can even start experimenting with it here: ([link to site](https://en-roads.climateinteractive.org/scenario.html?v=22.8.0)).

## Week  12  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: International Conservation Frameworks {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn about the major local, national, and multi-national approaches to reducing deforestation by comparing their efficacy and socioeconomic impacts. REDD and Payment for Ecosystem Services._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Dasgupta, Shreya. 2017 Does forest certification really work? _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2017/09/does-forest-certification-really-work/) 15 min.)

1. Gaworecki, Mike. (2017). Cash for conservation: Do payments for ecosystem services work? _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2017/10/cash-for-conservation-do-payments-for-ecosystem-services-work/) 15 min.)

1. Gaworecki, Mike. (2021). How effective is the EU’s marquee policy to reduce the illegal timber trade? _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2024/06/how-effective-is-the-eus-marquee-policy-to-reduce-the-illegal-timber-trade/) 15 min.)


<!-- - UN-REDD Programme: An introduction to REDD+ [link](https://www.un-redd.org/sites/default/files/2021-10/1.%20Introduction%20to%20REDD%2B_Tim%20Christophersen.pdf). -->

<!-- > Note: this is a slide deck designed to introduce the REDD+ framework to a general audience. The content is exceptional, but don't just read the material -- think about how it is being presented (graphics, etc.) and if it effectively communicates their message. I've asked you do this not just to prepare for today's lesson, but because it will be useful for your Final Project (TL;dr...the presentation is awful: busy slides, too much text, overly complicated graphics...we'll talk about how it could be improved). -->

<!-- redd+ projects are developed with communities, this is an example of why some might succeed or fail -->

<!-- The world’s new US$125 billion rainforest trust fund revives a 1990s idea – and shows its limits https://theconversation.com/the-worlds-new-us-125-billion-rainforest-trust-fund-revives-a-1990s-idea-and-shows-its-limits-270238 -->


#### Thursday: Trade in tropical commodities & DURIAN FEST {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn about the global market in tropical fruit crops and the economic impact of tropical fruit production in Florida._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Ryan, John Charles. (2018). Why the stinky durian really is the ‘king of all fruits’/ _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/why-the-stinky-durian-really-is-the-king-of-all-fruits-233872), 10 min).

1. Weintraub, Karen. (2019). They're Smelly and Spiky, and They Need Bats to Pollinate Them. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2019/12/04/science/bats-durians-indonesia.html), 5 min read).

1. Wharton, Rachel. (2020). How the Tip of Florida Became a Tropical-Fruit Paradise. Atlas Obscura. ([read online](https://www.atlasobscura.com/articles/tropical-fruit-farms-in-united-states), 5 min read).

1. NPR's Throughline Podcast (2022). There Will Be Bananas. [[listen online](https://www.npr.org/2020/01/07/794302086/there-will-be-bananas), 56 min].


<!-- - Frías, Carlos. (2022). Oddly colored and somewhat phallic: How Miami's rare tropical fruit went TikTok viral." Miami Herald. ([read online](https://www.miamiherald.com/miami-com/restaurants/article263608093.html#storylink=cpyhttps://www.miamiherald.com/miami-com/restaurants/article263608093.html), 5 min). -->


## Week  13  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Consumer choices & rain forest conservation {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To understand how global production and chains and consumer demand in Europe and North America influence patterns of deforestation in tropical countries._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 


1. Lawal,Shola. (2020). Our Endless Appetite For Chocolate Has Bitter Environmental Consequences. _Huffington Post_. ([read online](https://tinyurl.com/y6curgmn), 10 min read).

1. Mufson, Steven and Georges, Salwan. (2019). The trouble with chocolate. _Washington Post_ ([read online](https://www.washingtonpost.com/graphics/2019/national/climate-environment/mars-chocolate-deforestation-climate-change-west-africa/) to see the amazing maps, pictures, and data visualizations).

1. Williams, Wyatt. (2021). How Your Cup of Coffee Is Clearing the Jungle. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/2021/08/11/magazine/indonesia-rainforest-coffee.html), or listen to the audio version with the link at top of the article).

1. Carodenuto, Sophia. (2021). Chocolate fix: How the cocoa industry could end deforestation in West Africa. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/chocolate-fix-how-the-cocoa-industry-could-end-deforestation-in-west-africa-161953), 10 min read).

1. Mary Gagen. 2024. The world’s business and finance sectors can do much more to reverse deforestation – here’s the data to prove it. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/the-worlds-business-and-finance-sectors-can-do-much-more-to-reverse-deforestation-heres-the-data-to-prove-it-224392), 10 min).


#### Thursday: Community based conservation {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn about how local communities are engaged in rain forest conservation and sustainable development efforts in the tropics and beyond._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 


1. Katrina Kosec et al. (2025) Forest loss in Malawi: how having women at the table affected debates and decisions about solutions. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/forest-loss-in-malawi-how-having-women-at-the-table-affected-debates-and-decisions-about-solutions-research-259699), 10 min read).

1. _BBC World Service_ (2020). Don't underestimate what one person can do on their own: The man who grew his own rainforest. ([link to video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AngaeIf78AQ), 5 min long).

1. Dasgupta, Shreya. (2017). Does community-based forest management work in the tropics? _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2017/11/does-community-based-forest-management-work-in-the-tropics/), 15 min read).

1.  Maclean, Ruth with additional reporting by Caleb Kabanda and photography by Nanna Heitmann. (2022). What do the protectors of Congo's peatlands get in return?" _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2022/02/21/headway/peatlands-congo-climate-change.html), 10 min read).

<!-- - **Come prepared to make some money:** we're looking into the economic benefits of setting aside land (or not) for conservation. To prepare, review this document over before class: ([link](https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/00220485.2016.1146098), 10-15 min read). -->



## Week  14  {.unnumbered}

***Reading/Viewing:*** None (Thanksgiving Holiday)

## Week  15  {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Protected areas {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn about different global categories of protected areas, the importance of protected areas in the tropics for conserving forest, and how the threats to protected areas vary regionally and globally._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Kimbrough, L. (2021). How settlers, scientists, and a women-led industry saved Brazil's rarest primate". _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2021/05/how-settlers-scientists-and-a-women-led-industry-saved-brazils-rarest-primate/), 10 min read).

1. Dasgupta, Shreya (with research by Annika Schlemm & Zuzana Burivalova). (2017). Do protected areas work in the tropics?" Mongabay. ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2017/12/do-protected-areas-work-in-the-tropics/), 25 min read).

1. Matti Barthel et al. (2025) DRC’s plan for the world’s largest tropical forest reserve would be good for the planet: can it succeed? _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/drcs-plan-for-the-worlds-largest-tropical-forest-reserve-would-be-good-for-the-planet-can-it-succeed-254394), 10 min read).


#### Thursday: Forest restoration & Regeneration {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn the difference between passive regeneration and active restoration and assess evidence for whether they can be used to reverse the effects of deforestation._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Gaworecki, Mike. (2021). Is planting trees as good for the Earth as everyone says? _Mongabay_ ([read online](https://news.mongabay.com/2021/05/is-planting-trees-as-good-for-the-earth-as-everyone-says/) 15 min.)

1. Medici, Patricia. (2015). The coolest animal you know nothing about...and how we can save it. _TED Fellows Talk_. ([link to video](https://www.ted.com/talks/patricia_medici_the_coolest_animal_you_know_nothing_about_and_how_we_can_save_it#t-694653), 11:20 min long).

1. Robin Chazdon et al. (2021). Tropical forests can recover surprisingly quickly on deforested lands – and letting them regrow naturally is an effective and low‑cost way to slow climate change. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/tropical-forests-can-recover-surprisingly-quickly-on-deforested-lands-and-letting-them-regrow-naturally-is-an-effective-and-low-cost-way-to-slow-climate-change-173302), 10 min.).

<!-- - "Do tapirs defecate in the woods?" (2019). The Economist ([read online](https://www.economist.com/science-and-technology/2019/04/17/do-tapirs-defecate-in-the-woods), 5 min read). -->


## Week  16 {.unnumbered}

#### Tuesday: Tropical rain forests & Global Health {.unnumbered}

_**Goal:** To learn about the relationship between deforestation and the emergence and spread of tropical diseases like Zika and Malaria from the tropics to other regions of the globe._

***Reading/Viewing:*** 

1. Vittor, Amy, Gabriel Zorello Laporta, and Maria Anice Mureb Sallum. (2020). How deforestation helps deadly viruses jump from animals to humans. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/how-deforestation-helps-deadly-viruses-jump-from-animals-to-humans-139645), 15 min read).

1. UF EPI. (2024) Florida’s mosquitoes can make you sick: Here’s how to protect yourself. [read online](https://epi.ufl.edu/2024/05/01/floridas-mosquitoes-can-make-you-sick-heres-how-to-protect-yourself/), 10 min. read)

1. Kuchipudi, Suresh V. (2020). Why so many epidemics originate in Asia and Africa -- and why we can expect more. _The Conversation_. ([read online](https://theconversation.com/why-so-many-epidemics-originate-in-asia-and-africa-and-why-we-can-expect-more-131657), 10 min).








<!-- Chopping down areas of tropical rainforest is causing rising temperatures linked to thousands of deaths: https://theconversation.com/chopping-down-areas-of-tropical-rainforest-is-causing-rising-temperatures-linked-to-thousands-of-deaths-278737 -->

<!-- James Barrat, & Tangled Bank Studios (Producers), & Barrat, J. (Director). (2016). Spillover: Zika, Ebola and Beyond. [Video/DVD] Public Broadcasting Service. Retrieved from https://video.alexanderstreet.com/watch/spillover-zika-ebola-and-beyond -->


<!-- ## Week  15-2 Media coverage of Rain Forests  -->

<!-- _**Goal:** To learn how journalists based in different countries or different global audiences chose and cover stories about rain forests and deal with the risks of covering this beat*. -->

<!-- - Nicas, Jack (reporting) and Moriyama,Victor (Photos, Video). (2022). Inside the Amazon Journey That Left a Journalist and an Activist Dead. _New York Times_. ([read online](https://www.nytimes.com/interactive/2022/07/11/world/americas/amazon-murder-dom-phillips-bruno-pereira.html?smid=tw-nytimes&smtyp=cur), 15 min read) -->
